title: Cupia macrophylla
taxonomy: Cupia macrophylla is classified within the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Cupia. The classification of Cupia macrophylla is further detailed at the species level as Cupia macrophylla. However, the classification of this plant may vary depending on the source and the ongoing process of taxonomic updates. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification of Cupia macrophylla.
uses: Cupia macrophylla is traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits. It is reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating various health conditions. The plant is also used to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. Additionally, Cupia macrophylla is believed to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. However, further research is needed to fully confirm its medicinal uses and potential side effects.
